title: Optical engine
abstract: Described examples include an optical device (212), having a light source (202) with a light source output and a light integrator (204) having a light integrator input and a light integrator output, the light integrator input optically coupled to the light source output, and the light integrator (204) configured to provide divergent light at the light integrator output responsive to the light at the light source output. The optical device (212) also has: projection optics (214) with an optics input and an optics output, the projection optics (214) configured to project output light at the optics output responsive to modulated light at the optics input, in which a focal point of the optics input matches a divergence of the modulated light; and a spatial light modulator (210) optically coupled between the light integrator output and the optics input. The spatial light modulator (210) is configured to provide the modulated light responsive to the divergent light.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An optical device, comprising: a light source having a light source output, the light source configured to provide light at the light source output; a light integrator having a light integrator input and a light integrator output, the light integrator input optically coupled to the light source output, and the light integrator configured to provide divergent light at the light integrator output responsive to the light at the light source output; projection optics having an optics input and an optics output, the projection optics configured to project projection output light at the optics output responsive to a modulated light at the optics input, in which a focal point of the optics input matches a divergence of the modulated light; and a spatial light modulator optically coupled between the light integrator output and the optics input, the spatial light modulator configured to provide the modulated light responsive to the divergent light. 2. The optical device of claim 1, wherein the light integrator input has an input form factor that matches a form factor of the light source output and the light source output is proximate to the light integrator input. 3. The optical device of claim 1, further comprising a reverse total internal reflection prism configured to pass the divergent light through a face of the reverse total internal reflection prism before reaching the spatial light modulator, and configured to reflect the modulated light off the face before entering the optics input of the projection optics. 4. The optical device of claim 3, further comprising a cover prism between the light integrator and the reverse total internal reflection prism, wherein the cover prism has a surface perpendicular to a propagation path of the divergent light. 5. The optical device of claim 1, wherein the light source is a light emitting diode. 6. The optical device of claim 1, wherein the light integrator is a light tunnel. 7. The optical device of claim 1, wherein the divergent light has a pattern corresponding to a form factor of the spatial light modulator. 8. The optical device of claim 1, wherein the projection optics includes five lenses. 9. A three-dimensional printer, comprising: a vat having a transparent bottom and configured to contain a photo-polymerizing resin; a lift plate movably positioned within the vat; and an optical device configured to project a pattern of light through the transparent bottom, the optical device further including: a light source having a light source output, the light source configured to provide light at the light source output; a light integrator having a light integrator input and a light integrator output, the light integrator input optically coupled to the light source output, and the light integrator configured to provide divergent light at the light integrator output responsive to the light at the light source output; projection optics having an optics input and an optics output, the projection optics configured to project projection output light at the optics output through the transparent bottom responsive to a modulated light at the optics input, in which a focal point of the optics input matches a divergence of the modulated light; and a spatial light modulator optically coupled between the light integrator output and the optics input, the spatial light modulator configured to provide the modulated light responsive to the divergent light. 10. The three-dimensional printer of claim 9, wherein the light integrator input has an input form factor that matches an output form factor of the light source output and the light source output is proximate to the light integrator input. 11. The three-dimensional printer of claim 9, wherein the lift plate is positioned and moved by a control arm. 12. The three-dimensional printer of claim 9, further comprising a reverse total internal reflection prism configured to pass the divergent light through a face of the reverse total internal reflection prism before reaching the spatial light modulator, and configured to reflect the modulated light off the face before entering the optics input of the projection optics. 13. The three-dimensional printer of claim 12, further comprising a cover prism between the light integrator and the reverse total internal reflection prism, wherein the cover prism has a surface perpendicular to a propagation path of the divergent light. 14. The three-dimensional printer of claim 9, wherein the light source is a light emitting 

 diode. 15. The three-dimensional printer of claim 9, wherein the light integrator is a light tunnel. 16. The three-dimensional printer of claim 9, wherein the divergent light has a pattern corresponding to a form factor of the spatial light modulator. 17. The three-dimensional printer of claim 9, wherein the projection optics includes five lenses. 18. A method, comprising: directing light from a source of light through a light integrator, wherein the light integrator provides divergent output light to a spatial light modulator; and reflecting the divergent output light off the spatial light modulator to projection optics, the projection optics having an input focal point corresponding to a divergence of the divergent output light and having an output focal point at a target. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the projection optics receives the divergent output light reflected off the spatial light modulator via a reverse total internal reflection prism. 20. The method of claim 18, wherein the source of light is a light emitting diode.
